articles.title: Central Bank of Tunisia Holds Benchmark Rate at 8%
articles.brief: The Banque Centrale de Tunisie maintained its policy rate for the third consecutive quarter, citing persistent inflationary pressures from energy imports and weak dinar pass-through effects. Analysts expect no change until Q3 2026 at the earliest.

articles.title: Mistral Releases 72B Parameter Open Model Under Apache 2.0
articles.brief: The Paris-based AI lab Mistral published weights for its largest open model under a permissive Apache 2.0 licence, directly challenging proprietary frontier models from OpenAI and Anthropic. The release reignites debate over the safety and strategic implications of unrestricted open-weight releases.

articles.title: Libya Ceasefire Talks Resume in Tunis With Five New Delegations
articles.brief: UN-backed negotiations between Libyan rival factions resumed at the Tunis Forum, with five previously absent stakeholder groups participating for the first time since 2024. Diplomatic sources describe the session as 'cautiously productive' but warn that structural disagreements remain unresolved.

articles.title: IMF Raises MENA Growth Forecast to 3.1% in Spring World Economic Outlook
articles.brief: The International Monetary Fund revised its regional growth projection upward from 2.7%, citing stronger Gulf domestic consumption, improved oil revenues, and fiscal reform progress in Egypt and Morocco. Tunisia's individual outlook was revised modestly upward to 1.8% for 2026.

articles.title: Only 12% of Global Critical Infrastructure Has Begun Post-Quantum Migration
articles.brief: A comprehensive audit commissioned by ENISA found that the vast majority of critical infrastructure operators — including energy grids, water systems, and financial networks — have not begun implementing NIST-approved post-quantum cryptographic standards. Security researchers warn that harvest-now, decrypt-later attacks may already be underway.

articles.title: G7 Finance Ministers Hold Emergency Session on Bond Market Volatility
articles.brief: Finance ministers from G7 nations convened an emergency virtual session following a sharp selloff in sovereign bond markets that pushed yields to levels not seen since 2008. The session focused on coordinated communication strategy rather than direct market intervention.

articles.title: AfCFTA Digital Trade Protocol Reaches Ratification Quorum After Rwanda Signs
articles.brief: Rwanda became the 22nd African Union member state to ratify the AfCFTA Digital Trade Protocol, triggering its entry into force and establishing the continent's first binding legal framework for cross-border e-commerce. Implementation will begin in a phased rollout starting January 2027.

articles.title: Sfax Industrial Zone Launches Nearshoring Incentive Package Targeting European Firms
articles.brief: The Tunisian government unveiled a comprehensive incentive package for its expanded Sfax industrial zone, targeting European manufacturers seeking to reduce Asia-Pacific supply chain dependence following the 2025 tariff disruptions. Benefits include 10-year tax holidays, subsidised logistics infrastructure, and fast-track permits.
